title: Yacht Rental in Dubai: Costs, Marinas and Seasons 2026
html:
Yacht rental in Dubai puts the Palm Jumeirah skyline within reach, with most boats leaving from Dubai Marina or Dubai Harbour and day prices that typically run from around AED 2,500 to well over AED 15,000 by size. BoatHire24 lists verified yachts across the emirate for couples, families and groups.

Where to set off in Dubai

Dubai gives you several genuine starting points, each with its own view of the coast. Most rentals cluster around a few well-run marinas, so it helps to know which suits you.

  • Dubai Marina: the busiest hub, ringed by towers and restaurants, ideal for a first charter.
  • Dubai Harbour: a deep-water marina beside Bluewaters Island and Ain Dubai.
  • Palm Jumeirah: departures run quickly past Atlantis and the fronds.
  • Jumeirah and the Burj Al Arab coastline: a short hop south for the sail-shaped hotel.
  • The World Islands: roughly 4 nautical miles offshore, a calm anchorage for swimming.

Which boat to rent in Dubai?

The right boat depends on your group size, your budget and whether you want speed, shade or space to host. Dubai's fleet spans nimble open boats through to crewed motor yachts.

  1. Open speedboat: best for sightseeing sprints past the Palm, seats up to 8, from AED 600 per hour.
  2. Sport cruiser: good for couples on a half-day, seats up to 10, from AED 3,000.
  3. Flybridge motor yacht: shade and a saloon for groups, seats up to 12, from AED 5,000.
  4. Luxury crewed yacht: built for celebrations, seats up to 20, from AED 9,000.
  5. Catamaran: the steadiest deck for swimming, seats up to 25, from AED 7,000.

To feel the engines rather than lounge, the QuickSilver class fits, and you can study the layout on the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing before you commit. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Dubai or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dubai?

Expect roughly AED 200 per seat for a shared tour and several thousand dirhams for a private yacht across a day. Prices rise with length, crew and season, and operators quote in AED before extras.

  • A shared sightseeing tour costs from AED 150 to AED 250 per person for around 90 minutes.
  • A small private motorboat for a half-day runs from AED 2,500 to AED 4,000.
  • A catamaran for a full day lands between AED 7,000 and AED 12,000.
  • A crewed luxury yacht with a skipper starts near AED 9,000 and climbs with size.

You can compare live rates and dates for yacht charter Dubai across the listed fleet, weighing an hourly hop against a full day before you pay.

How does booking a boat in Dubai work?

You choose your dates, confirm the boat and skipper online, then meet the crew at the marina on the day. Booking ahead matters in peak weeks, when the best yachts sell out fast.

Most charters in Dubai are skippered, so you need no personal licence. The operator holds the commercial permissions and briefs you on safety, while self-drive bareboat hire is rarer here.

When is the best season to sail in Dubai?

The high season runs from October to April, when temperatures ease and the Gulf is at its most comfortable. Summer stays sailable but very hot, so many visitors prefer evening cruises then.

The prevailing breeze here is the Shamal, a north-westerly that can pick up over the Gulf and raise a short chop. For self-drive boats the country principle is that the UAE requires recognised qualifications, and the operator confirms exact requirements for any bareboat hire.

Book a morning slot between November and March for the calmest water and clearest skyline photographs, and pack a light layer, because the breeze offshore feels cooler than the marina suggests.

At a glance

  • Price band: AED 600 per hour to AED 15,000-plus per day
  • Peak months: October to April
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to a full day
  • Capacity: 6 to 25 guests depending on the yacht
  • Currency: United Arab Emirates dirham, AED
  • Reference year: 2026

Questions about boat rental in Dubai

Do I need a licence to rent a yacht in Dubai?

Most Dubai yachts come with a professional skipper, so guests need no licence at all. You join the crew at the marina, and they handle navigation, mooring and the safety briefing.

How many people can a Dubai charter carry?

Capacity varies by hull, but typical boats here seat between 6 and 25 guests. Smaller open boats suit up to 8, while crewed yachts and catamarans host groups of 20 plus.

Can I see dolphins or visit the World Islands?

Many Dubai charters include a swimming stop near the World Islands, roughly 4 nautical miles offshore, and dolphins are sometimes spotted in open water nearby. Ask your operator to route around the conditions.

How far ahead should I book?

For weekends and the October to April peak, booking two to three weeks ahead is wise. Popular yachts and sunset slots fill quickly, so early confirmation secures the boat and price.

Whether you want a fast hour past the Palm or a full day at anchor near the World Islands, Dubai rewards planning, and the listed fleet covers every budget in AED. Compare boats and dates, then book your day on the Gulf.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubai and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
